chapters.title: Biasing Techniques
chapters.content: Hello student! Today we learn about biasing. Bias means giving transistor a 'job' - set its working point so it works properly. Like setting a fan speed before using it.

derivations.title: h-Parameters from Hybrid Model
derivations.formula: Vbe = hie Ib + hre Vce, Ic = hfe Ib + hoe Vce

derivations.title: Stability Factor S
steps: For voltage divider bias: S = (β+1)/(β+1 + Rth/Re)
Lower S = better stability against β variation
Fixed bias: S = β+1 (very poor stability)

derivations.title: Emitter Current Derivation
steps: KVL at base: Vth = Ib×Rth + Vbe + Ie×Re

derivations.title: h-Parameter Conversion Between Configurations
steps: hie = hib × (β+1) for CE from CB
hre = 1 - hfb for CE from CB
hoe = hoe × (β+1) for CE from CB

numericalProblems.question: Design CE amplifier bias: VCE=10V, IC=2mA, β=100, VBE=0.7V using voltage divider
numericalProblems.given: R2=10kΩ, Vcc=15V. Find R1, Rc, Re for stability S<10
numericalProblems.answer: R1≈100Ω, Rc≈3.4kΩ, Re≈6.6kΩ (adjust for valid VCE)

numericalProblems.question: Calculate stability factor S for fixed bias vs voltage divider bias
numericalProblems.given: β=100, Rb=200kΩ, Re=1kΩ for voltage divider, Rth=20kΩ

numericalProblems.question: Find h-parameters from datasheet: β=150, IC=1mA → hie=?

quiz.q: What is stability factor S formula for voltage divider?

chapters.tanglishNotes: Indha biasing-ula, transistor-ku job kudukkradhu. Vth, Rth calculate pannanum. Stability factor S-um kammi irundha nalla irukum. hie na input impedance, hfe na current gain. Indha formulas ROMba important: S=(β+1)/(β+1+Rth/Re).

chapters.title: Amplifiers (Single & Multistage)
chapters.content: Amplifiers make signals bigger. CE (Common Emitter) gives high gain but inverts signal. CC (Common Collector) is emitter follower with gain≈1 but high input impedance.

derivations.title: Voltage Gain Av for CE Amplifier
Input Impedance Ri for CE
steps: For CC: Ric = hie + (1+hfe)×Re (high input Z)

derivations.title: Current Gain Ai for CE
steps: With source: Ais = Ai × Zin/(Zin + Rs)

derivations.title: Overall Gain for 2-Stage Amplifier
steps: With loading: Av2_loaded = Av2 × Zin2/(Zin2 + Rout1)
Overall current gain: Ai_total = Ai1 × Ai2
Power gain: Ap = Av × Ai × cosφ

numericalProblems.question: Calculate Av, Ai, Ri, Ro for CE amplifier: hie=1.1k, hfe=50, hoe=25μS, Rc=2k, RL=2k, Rs=1k
Design 2-stage amplifier with total gain >1000, Av1=-50, find Av2 and Rc2

quiz.q: What is approximate voltage gain formula for CE?

chapters.tanglishNotes: Indha amplifier-la, signal-a peridhu peridhu pannum. CE configuration-la gain negative-ah irukum (phase reversal). Av = -hfe×RL'/hie nu formula use pannanum. Ri na input impedance, Ro na output impedance. 2-stage amplifier-ula total gain = Av1×Av2.

chapters.title: Feedback Amplifiers
chapters.content: Feedback means taking some output and feeding it back to input. Negative feedback reduces gain but improves stability, bandwidth, and reduces distortion.

derivations.title: Gain with Feedback Af
steps: A = open-loop gain, β = feedback factor

derivations.title: Input Impedance with Feedback
steps: For voltage-series: input Z increases with feedback
For current-shunt: input Z decreases with feedback
derivations.formula: Zif = Zi(1+Aβ) for series, Zif = Zi/(1+Aβ) for shunt

derivations.title: Output Impedance with Feedback
steps: For voltage-series: output Z decreases (better regulation)
derivations.formula: Zof = Zo/(1+Aβ) for voltage, Zof = Zo(1+Aβ) for current

derivations.title: Barkhausen Criterion for Oscillation
steps: Phase shift oscillator: 3 RC stages × 60° each = 180°

derivations.title: Desensitivity D
steps: Larger Aβ = less gain variation with temperature
Gain variation reduced by factor D

numericalProblems.question: Calculate Af, Rif, Rof for voltage-series feedback: A=-100, β=0.1, Ri=1k, Ro=5k
Find phase shift at f=10kHz for RC phase shift oscillator (3 stages)
numericalProblems.given: Each RC stage: φ = -arctan(2πfRC), R=10k, C=0.01μF
numericalProblems.solution: f₀=1/(2πRC√6) = 1/(2π×10k×0.01μ×2.45) = 650Hz. At 10kHz: φ per stage ≈ -arctan(10k/650) ≈ -86.3°. 3 stages = -258.9° ≈ -180° (oscillates)

chapters.tanglishNotes: Feedback-ula, output-ai eduthu input-ku kudukkrom. Negative feedback-na gain kammi aagum, aana stable aagum. Af = A/(1+Aβ) nu formula. Barkhausen criterion: |Aβ|=1 and phase=180° na oscillation aagum.

chapters.title: Oscillators
chapters.content: Oscillators generate AC signals without input. RC phase shift uses resistors and capacitors. LC oscillators (Hartley/Colpitts) use inductors and capacitors for high frequency.

derivations.title: Frequency for RC Phase Shift Oscillator
steps: Each stage phase shift: φ = arctan(1/2πfRC)

derivations.title: Frequency for Hartley Oscillator
steps: L_eq = L1 + L2 + 2M (with mutual inductance M)

derivations.title: Frequency for Colpitts Oscillator
steps: Voltage divider using C1, C2 provides feedback

derivations.title: Crystal Oscillator Q-Factor
steps: Crystal equivalent: L, C1, R, C0 (motional params)

numericalProblems.question: Design RC phase shift oscillator for f₀=1kHz, R=10kΩ, find C
Calculate Hartley oscillator frequency: L1=1mH, L2=1mH, C=0.01μF (no mutual coupling)

quiz.q: What is RC phase shift oscillator frequency formula?

chapters.tanglishNotes: Indha oscillator-ula, input illama output signal kudukkum. RC phase shift-ula 3-stage RC ladder use pannuvom. f₀ = 1/(2πRC√6) nu nyabagam vechukonga. Hartley-ula L1+L2 use pannuvom, Colpitts-ula C1||C2 use pannuvom.

chapters.title: Op-Amps, Power Amps & Supplies
chapters.content: Op-amps are versatile ICs that perform mathematical operations. Inverting amplifier flips signal, non-inverting keeps phase same. Class B power amps use two transistors for better efficiency.

derivations.title: Non-Inverting Amplifier Gain
steps: Voltage at inverting terminal: V- = V_out×R1/(R1+Rf)

derivations.title: Class A Maximum Efficiency
steps: Practical: η ≈ 25-40% due to V_sat, Re

numericalProblems.question: Design inverting amplifier: Av=-10, Rin=1kΩ → find Rf
Calculate Class B power: Vcc=15V, RL=8Ω → find P_out, P_DC, η

quiz.q: What is inverting amplifier gain formula?
What is maximum efficiency of Class B?

chapters.tanglishNotes: Op-amp-ula romba useful IC. Inverting amplifier-la output phase reverse aagum: Av = -Rf/R1. Non-inverting-la phase same: Av = 1+Rf/R1. Class A efficiency 50%, Class B efficiency 78.5%. LM317 regulator-ula V_out = 1.25×(1+R2/R1).
